existingClaudeMdInstructions: Use 'babysitter call' or 'babysit' for orchestrating complex workflows
Focus on professional photo management features (tagging, face rec, search, culling)
Maintain clean code standards with proper tests
Build for Windows first, then optimize (CUDA/Vulkan support)
UX should be intuitive for professional photographers
Autonomous operation - let babysitter handle the orchestration

architecture.dataFlow: Electron app launches Python FastAPI backend (via npm start script), frontend (HTML/JS) communicates with backend via HTTP API (localhost:8765), backend uses SQLite for storage and llama.cpp-compatible endpoints for AI features

modules.description: Electron desktop app shell - main process and preload scripts
modules.name: electron

modules.description: Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS frontend served by Electron
modules.name: frontend

modules.description: Python FastAPI backend with AI services, photo management, and SQLite database
modules.name: backend

modules.description: Application icons and icon generation scripts
modules.name: icons

architecture.pattern: Desktop application with separate backend service (Electron + Python FastAPI)

bottlenecks.description: Build system instability - multiple commits fixing Nuitka, electron-builder, Windows-specific issues
Windows build challenges with DLL dependencies and icon handling

claudeMdInstructions: Use 'babysitter call' or 'babysit' for orchestrating complex workflows
Focus on professional photo management features (tagging, face rec, search, culling)
Maintain clean code standards with proper tests
Build for Windows first, then optimize (CUDA/Vulkan support)
UX should be intuitive for professional photographers
Autonomous operation - let babysitter handle the orchestration

additionalRules: German comments in Python backend code allowed
English comments in JavaScript frontend code
API endpoints follow RESTful conventions with /api/ prefix
All documentation in English (migrated from German)
Clean code standards - as expected from a professional developer
Never mention 'Excire Foto 2027' or similar inspiration sources in code or docs

codeStyle.javascript: Vanilla JS, no framework, clean and modular
codeStyle.python: Standard Python conventions, German comments allowed

conventions.errorHandling: Try-except blocks in Python, try-catch in JavaScript, HTTP exception handling via FastAPI

git.commitFormat: Conventional commits (ci:, fix:, feat:, docs:, Translate:)

naming.javascript: camelCase for functions/variables, PascalCase for classes
naming.python: snake_case for functions/variables, PascalCase for classes

conventions.testingConventions: Comprehensive tests required (pytest for Python, Jest/Vitest for JS when added)

profile.description: Open-source AI-powered photo management for professional photographers. Desktop application with Electron frontend and Python/FastAPI backend. Features AI auto-tagging, face recognition, semantic search, culling workflow, and aesthetic rating. UX inspired by professional photo management tools with improved workflow.

goals.description: Erstellung und Verbesserung von FotoDerp - erste Testversion für Windows
Vollständiger Feature-Set: AI-Tagging, Face Recognition, Semantic Search, Similarity Search, Culling Workflow, Aesthetic Rating, OCR, Collections, Analytics
Sauberer Code, Tests, Profi-Standards (wie von einem professionellen Entwickler erwartet)
Multi-Platform Support (Linux, macOS, Windows) mit stabilen Builds
UX orientiert an professionellen Foto-Verwaltungs-Tools, aber besser - intuitive Bedienung für Profi-Fotografen

painPoints.description: Complex build process with Nuitka compilation and multi-platform electron-builder
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Stabilize build system, add comprehensive tests for build process

painPoints.description: Windows builds require special handling (Dependencies, DLL management)
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Create dedicated Windows build workflow with proper dependency management

painPoints.description: CI pipeline required multiple rewrites to stabilize
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Use babysitter for CI/CD automation and testing

painPoints.description: Entwicklung stoppt weil das Model stoppt
painPoints.suggestedRemediation: Autonomous operation with babysitter orchestration - no manual intervention required

workflows.name: development

workflows.description: Build distributable packages for multiple platforms
workflows.name: build

workflows.description: Direct commits to main branch (single developer workflow)
workflows.name: trunk-based

justifications.codebase-analyzer: Analyse der bestehenden Codebasis für Verbesserungen
justifications.gsd/execute: Ausführung der Entwicklungsaufgaben mit autonomer Operation
justifications.gsd/iterative-convergence: Iterative Qualitätsverbesserung für sauberen Code und Tests
justifications.gsd/new-project: Für die Erstellung der ersten Testversion für Windows - strukturierte Projektplanung
justifications.gsd/plan: Detaillierte Planung für Features und UX-Verbesserungen
justifications.gsd/verify: Verifizierung der Features gegen die Anforderungen (professional standards)
justifications.methodologies/TDD: Test-Driven Development für professionelle Code-Qualität - wie von einem Profi erwartet
justifications.specializations/ux-ui-design: UX-Orientierung an professionellen Foto-Verwaltungs-Tools (ohne den Namen zu erwähnen)
justifications.test-generator: Automatische Testgenerierung für Python (pytest) und JS
justifications.ui-ux-reviewer: Sicherstellen, dass die UX intuitiv für Profi-Fotografen ist

toolSelection.recommendedMethodology: TDD (Test-Driven Development) mit iterative-convergence für qualitätssicherung

remediations.recommendation: Verwende gsd/execute mit Fokus auf Build-Stabilisierung und Tests
Dediziertes Windows-Build mit proper dependency management via gsd/plan + execute
Autonome Operation mit babysitter - gsd/execute ohne manuelle Intervention

instructions: Read the existing CLAUDE.md file if it exists
Add a babysitter section (## Babysitter) if not already present
Include the following in the babysitter section:
- Recommended babysitter commands for this project
- Installed processes and how to invoke them
- Recommended methodology and when to use it
If recommended skills are available, document how to use them
If CI/CD was configured, note the CI/CD integration
Do NOT remove or modify existing CLAUDE.md content
Add the new content in a clearly marked section
Use markdown formatting consistent with the existing file
Prepare the additions as a separate artifact so the user can review
Extract key instructions to be stored in the profile claudeMdInstructions array
Actually update the CLAUDE.md file with the additions
prompt.outputFormat: JSON with updated (boolean), claudeMdPath (string), addedSections (array of section names), claudeMdInstructions (array of instruction strings), additions (string of markdown added)
prompt.role: technical writer and Claude Code configuration specialist
prompt.task: Update the project CLAUDE.md to include babysitter process instructions, recommended commands, and project-specific babysitter configuration.

description: Add babysitter processes, commands, and instructions to the project CLAUDE.md file
title: Update CLAUDE.md with babysitter instructions
